items.text:
Fall Textbook Buyback — New Location
December 8 to December 12

The campus bookstore will hold its end-of-semester textbook buyback during finals week. Please note that the event has been moved from the bookstore floor to Room 118 in the Harmon Student Center, where longer counter hours can be offered.

Buyback hours are 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM each day. To sell a book, students must present a valid student ID and the book itself. Cash payment of up to $50.00 per title will be issued on the spot; titles worth more than that amount are paid by check, which is mailed within five business days.

Please note the following:
- Books with water damage or missing pages cannot be accepted.
- Lab manuals and workbooks that have been written in are not eligible.
- Editions older than the one listed for spring courses are valued at a reduced rate.

Questions about a specific title may be directed to the buyback desk at buyback@harmon.edu before December 5.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must a student bring in order to sell a textbook?

options.A: A receipt and the original packaging
options.B: A valid student ID and the book
options.C: A course list and a check stub
options.D: A lab manual and a workbook

questions.explanation: The notice states that to sell a book, students must present a valid student ID and the book itself. The other options recombine campus-sounding terms (receipts, course lists, lab manuals) that are not the stated requirement.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a textbook valued at more than $50.00?

options.A: It will be rejected at the buyback desk
options.B: It will be paid for by mailed check
options.C: It will be sold on the bookstore floor
options.D: It will be exchanged for store credit

questions.explanation: The notice says cash of up to $50.00 per title is issued on the spot, while titles worth more than that amount are paid by check mailed within five business days. So a book above $50.00 would not be paid in cash but by mailed check. The other options reverse or fabricate the payment rule.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To list the textbooks required for spring courses
options.B: To explain the rules for course final examinations
options.C: To announce a relocated textbook buyback event
options.D: To invite students to apply for bookstore jobs

questions.explanation: The notice announces the end-of-semester buyback, emphasizes the move to Room 118, and gives hours, payment terms, and eligibility rules. Its overall purpose is to announce the relocated buyback event, not to list spring textbooks, explain exam rules, or advertise jobs.

items.text:
Subject: Covering my Friday library shift

Hi Marcus,

I hope your week is going well. I'm scheduled to work the circulation desk this Friday, September 19, from 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM, but I have a dentist appointment that I couldn't move. Would you be able to take the shift for me?

If you can cover it, I'd be happy to work your Sunday morning shift next weekend in return. Either way, we both need to log the swap in the staff portal by Thursday at noon so that Ms. Okafor can approve it before payroll closes. Without her approval the hours won't be paid correctly.

Let me know by tomorrow if you can do it, and I'll enter the request right away. Thanks so much for considering it.

Thanks,
Dana

questions.stem: According to the email, why is Dana unable to work the Friday shift?

options.A: She has been moved to a Sunday shift
options.B: She must close the staff portal early
options.C: She has a dentist appointment that day
options.D: She has to meet with Ms. Okafor

questions.explanation: Dana writes that she is scheduled for Friday but has a dentist appointment she couldn't move, which is why she asks Marcus to cover. The other options reuse real terms (Sunday shift, staff portal, Ms. Okafor) but attach them to the wrong reason.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the swap if it is not logged by Thursday at noon?

options.A: Dana will have to work the shift anyway
options.B: Marcus will be moved to Sunday mornings
options.C: The hours may not be paid correctly
options.D: The circulation desk will stay closed

questions.explanation: Dana explains that the swap must be logged by Thursday at noon so Ms. Okafor can approve it before payroll closes, and that without her approval the hours won't be paid correctly. So missing the deadline risks the hours not being paid correctly. The other options are not supported by the email.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To ask a coworker to cover a work shift
options.B: To report a problem with the staff portal
options.C: To confirm a payroll deadline with a manager
options.D: To schedule a dentist appointment for Friday

questions.explanation: The email's central request is for Marcus to take Dana's Friday circulation shift, with the portal logging and payroll details offered as supporting conditions. Its main purpose is to ask a coworker to cover a shift, not to report a portal problem, confirm payroll, or arrange an appointment.
